metric_value: Intranet-first: real Internet value lies in internal use of Internet protocols; build architectural foundation before external deployment
notes: Aberdeen's central thesis: Intranet as proving ground, immediate benefit source, and long-term foundation

metric_value: Most enterprise data in plain-vanilla format; must define web-presentable data elements and user-friendly interface
notes: Content gap between text-format web and enterprise database data

metric_value: Web browser/server become bottlenecks as demand grows; must use TP-monitor-like middleware to bypass
notes: Stateless HTTP + session-oriented data = fundamental scalability conflict

metric_value: No vendor has shrink-wrapped solution for all enterprise Internet needs; require Internet-enabled CADEs for customization
notes: Aberdeen recommends second-generation CADEs with Internet/Java support

metric_value: Internet web servers lag enterprise systems in security, availability, and administration tools
notes: Aberdeen recommends Kerberos, firewalls, distributed database repositories

metric_value: Wide array of e-commerce solutions emerging; IS must move aggressively to integrate into architecture
notes: Order entry, payment, credit verification, EDI integration required

metric_value: 64-bit VLM (>4GB RAM) enables higher performance for data warehousing and mixed OLTP/DSS Internet applications
source_page: State of the Art: Server Hardware
notes: Digital, Sun, SGI, SNI/Pyramid delivering 64-bit solutions; Oracle, Sybase, Informix supporting

metric_value: NUMA extends SMP with clustering and MPP features; allows scaling beyond SMP limits without application changes
source_page: State of the Art: Server Hardware
notes: Sequent, Digital, Pyramid, Data General delivering NUMA hardware

metric_value: Informix + Illustra DataBlades object-relational solution to be merged by 1997
notes: Informix acquired Illustra in 1995; Aberdeen predicts integration by 1997

metric_value: Informix Universal Server (1996) integrated Illustra DataBlades; prediction accurate; Informix acquired by IBM 2001
notes: Aberdeen's timeline prediction proved correct; Informix eventually absorbed into IBM DB2

metric_name: Heterogeneous RDBMS replication by end of 1996
metric_value: Aberdeen anticipates most RDBMS vendors will support heterogeneous replication by end of 1996
notes: Required for Internet DB servers to replicate to multiple backend databases

metric_value: Oracle WebSystem: replacement web browser/server with load balancing and RDBMS integration; best when Oracle already in architecture
notes: Aberdeen classifies RDBMS-supplier middleware as appropriate only within same-vendor architectures

metric_value: Java is overhyped; lacks 4GL/VPE extensions; applets too large for 28.8K baud; 'C++ for the Internet' near-term
source_page: State of the Art: Development Tools
notes: Aberdeen 'flatly states' Java will deliver little in immediate future; Java support expected from most toolsets by end of 1996

metric_value: Most toolset suppliers will provide Java support by end of 1996; once CADEs add Java, they offer best-of-all-worlds
source_page: State of the Art: Development Tools
notes: Aberdeen bullish on Java+CADE combination while bearish on applets alone

metric_value: Java became dominant enterprise application language by 1999-2001 (J2EE); CADE+Java prediction partially correct but applets also declined as Aberdeen predicted
notes: Aberdeen's near-term Java skepticism accurate; long-term Java dominance via J2EE/servlets not fully anticipated

metric_value: Internet electronic commerce promises dramatic decreases in selling costs and new opportunities for customer interaction
notes: Aberdeen predicts e-commerce will be transformative for sales, supplier, and customer transactions

metric_value: E-commerce transformed retail, financial services, and B2B transactions globally; selling cost reductions materialized as predicted
notes: Amazon, eBay, Alibaba etc. validated Aberdeen's 1996 prediction completely

metric_value: Big telecom companies will not deliver major end-user bandwidth increases in near future; 2-way cable not ready
notes: Aberdeen cautions against expecting near-term bandwidth expansion

metric_value: Broadband (DSL, cable) rollout accelerated 1998-2002; Aberdeen's near-term constraint proved partially correct but underestimated speed of cable/DSL
notes: 2-way cable (cable modem) was ready by 1998-2000; Aberdeen slightly too pessimistic on timeline

metric_name: CA-Unicenter ICE for internet systems management
metric_value: CA-Unicenter Internet Commerce Enabled (ICE) exemplifies trend toward enterprise-class systems management of internet computing
source_page: State of the Art: Systems Management
notes: Aberdeen endorses CA-Unicenter ICE as leading enterprise internet management solution

metric_name: Middleware as most critical internet architecture component
metric_value: Of all Internet architectural components, middleware is the most critical to scalability
notes: Aberdeen's strongest prescriptive statement in the study; TP-monitor-style middleware is the linchpin

metric_value: Many second-generation CADEs will allow highly scalable Internet application development by mid-1996
source_page: State of the Art: Development Tools
notes: Aberdeen expects CADE tools to add Java support by mid-1996

metric_value: CADEs (PowerBuilder, Delphi, Forte etc.) added Java support by 1997-1998; CADEs largely displaced by Java IDE tools (Eclipse, NetBeans) by 2000
notes: Prediction directionally correct on timing; Java ultimately displaced CADEs rather than augmenting them
